project.name: vault-spider
project.description: JSON-only CLI (plus MCP server and Streamlit UI) for an Obsidian vault: hybrid retrieval over ChromaDB + BM25, cited answer synthesis with abstention, corpus health lint, plan-only enrichment, a golden eval harness, and safe note mutations executed through the running Obsidian app.
project.json_envelope: {ok: true, action, result, meta} | {ok: false, action, error}; always check `ok`, not just the exit code

generator_note: Structure extracted from the git-tracked source via Python ast; line numbers refer to the commit current on the generation date.

read_path: index.store (Chroma collection: document + section entries; BM25 corpus)
retrieval.searcher (BM25 + embeddings via llm.openrouter, fused by retrieval.fusion; thorough mode reranks)
compounding.distill (--save: write answer back as a distilled note)
write_path: cli.py mutation commands (create-note, edit-note, merge-frontmatter, add-links, insert-related, move-note, rename-note, open-note)
obsidian.notes (contract enforcement, dry-run diffs, sha256 compare-and-write)
running Obsidian app -> vault files (wikilinks update, plugins fire)
surfaces: bin/vault-spider -> vault_spider.cli (JSON envelope on stdout, exit 1 on error)
bin/vault-spider-mcp -> vault_spider.mcp_server (dual-transport MCP for Claude Desktop / ChatGPT)

invariants: Every CLI command prints exactly one JSON envelope: {ok: true, action, result, meta} or {ok: false, action, error}; check `ok`, not just the exit code.
Read path (sync, retrieve, lint) works on vault files directly; the write path goes only through the Obsidian CLI. Never write vault files directly from mutation code.
One note indexes as one document-granularity entry plus N section entries in a single Chroma collection (granularity metadata field). Section splitting is deterministic and heading-based.
`provenance` frontmatter (human | reference | llm | distilled) is set at note creation, immutable once set, never proposed by enrichment, and orthogonal to `type`.
`id`, `created`, and `provenance` are immutable once set; `lint --fix` writes only missing values, never edits existing ones.
Sync is failure-safe: old index entries are deleted only after all new embeddings are computed and validated.
Every mutating command accepts --dry-run and returns exactly what would change with meta.dry_run: true, making no backend calls.
Eval runs go against a dedicated --chroma-path, never the live-vault index.
Distilled notes are regenerable pointers to their sources; raw notes always win on conflict.

commands.summary: Print the machine-readable command + contract schema (version 2).
Incremental index sync: add new, re-embed changed/moved, delete removed notes.
Hybrid retrieval returning the scored-candidate contract. Filters: --folder --tag --type --provenance --since --until.
Retrieve then synthesize a cited answer; abstains when unsupported; --save persists a distilled note.
Plan-only enrichment: proposed title/frontmatter/links/placement as JSON; never mutates.
Read-only corpus health report; --fix writes only missing id/created/updated.
Golden-dataset validation and scored benchmark with a versioned results contract.

commands.command: create-note / read-note / edit-note / merge-frontmatter / add-links / insert-related / move-note / rename-note / open-note
commands.summary: Note mutations through the running Obsidian app; all mutating commands accept --dry-run.

vault_spider (root).role: Entry points and cross-cutting plumbing: the JSON-only CLI, the MCP server, configuration, the JSON envelope, and shared helpers.

modules.summary: JSON-only CLI for the vault: retrieval/synthesis plus safe note mutations.

classes.name: JsonArgumentParser
classes.doc: Convert argparse failures into the CLI's stable JSON error contract.

modules.summary: Configuration for Vault Spider retrieval and ranking.
JSON envelope helpers (bearctl pattern) for the vault-spider CLI.

classes.doc: Typed failure that maps 1:1 onto a failure envelope.

modules.summary: MCP server exposing Vault Spider's stable JSON CLI contracts.

classes.name: TextEdit
classes.doc: One exact-text replacement in a note body.

functions.name: vault_stats
functions.doc: Return statistics for the current Vault Spider index.

functions.name: sync_index
functions.signature: (root: Optional[str]=None, reset: bool=False, dry_run: bool=True) -> dict[str, Any]
functions.doc: Plan or run an incremental vault index sync. Defaults to a non-writing dry run.

functions.name: search_vault
functions.signature: (query: str, mode: Mode='fast', granularity: Granularity='document', limit: ResultLimit=10, folder: Optional[str]=None, tags: Optional[list[str]]=None, note_type: Optional[str]=None, provenance: Optional[Provenance]=None, since: Optional[str]=None, until: Optional[str]=None, must_include: Optional[list[str]]=None) -> dict[str, Any]
functions.doc: Hybrid-search indexed notes and return ranked candidates with score evidence.

functions.name: answer_from_vault
functions.signature: (query: str, mode: Mode='thorough', granularity: Granularity='mixed', limit: ResultLimit=10, context_notes: ContextLimit=8, folder: Optional[str]=None, tags: Optional[list[str]]=None, note_type: Optional[str]=None, provenance: Optional[Provenance]=None, since: Optional[str]=None, until: Optional[str]=None, must_include: Optional[list[str]]=None) -> dict[str, Any]
functions.doc: Answer from indexed notes with citations, abstaining when evidence is insufficient.

functions.name: lint_vault
functions.doc: Inspect vault health without modifying notes.

functions.name: plan_enrichment
functions.signature: (note: str, root: Optional[str]=None, intent: Optional[str]=None, source_url: Optional[str]=None, title: Optional[str]=None) -> dict[str, Any]
functions.doc: Propose metadata, links, and placement for an existing note without changing it.

functions.name: read_note
functions.doc: Read one vault-relative Markdown note through the running Obsidian app.

functions.name: edit_note
functions.signature: (path: str, edits: Annotated[list[TextEdit], Field(min_length=1)], expected_sha256: Optional[str]=None, dry_run: bool=True) -> dict[str, Any]
functions.doc: Edit a note body with exact replacements and an optimistic concurrency guard.

functions.name: create_note
functions.signature: (path: str, content: str='', frontmatter: Optional[dict[str, Any]]=None, auto_id: bool=True, dry_run: bool=True) -> dict[str, Any]
functions.doc: Create a note through Obsidian. Defaults to dry-run and refuses overwrites.

functions.name: merge_frontmatter
functions.signature: (path: str, patch: dict[str, Any], dry_run: bool=True) -> dict[str, Any]
functions.doc: Merge frontmatter through Obsidian. Defaults to dry-run; id and created stay immutable.

functions.name: add_links
functions.signature: (path: str, links: list[LinkSpec], dry_run: bool=True) -> dict[str, Any]
functions.doc: Turn matching note text into wikilinks through Obsidian. Defaults to dry-run.

functions.name: insert_related
functions.signature: (path: str, targets: list[str], dry_run: bool=True) -> dict[str, Any]
functions.doc: Add deduplicated wikilinks under a Related heading. Defaults to dry-run.

functions.name: move_note
functions.signature: (path: str, destination: str, dry_run: bool=True) -> dict[str, Any]
functions.doc: Move a note through Obsidian so incoming links update. Defaults to dry-run.

functions.name: rename_note
functions.signature: (path: str, name: str, dry_run: bool=True) -> dict[str, Any]
functions.doc: Rename a note through Obsidian so incoming links update. Defaults to dry-run.

functions.name: open_note
functions.doc: Open a vault-relative note in the running Obsidian app.

functions.name: create_server
functions.doc: Build the MCP server for stdio or Streamable HTTP transport.

vault_spider.corpus.role: Reads the vault: loads Markdown notes, parses frontmatter, resolves note identity (ULID or path hash), and splits notes into deterministic heading-based sections.

modules.summary: Deterministic section splitting for notes (no LLM).

functions.name: split_sections
functions.signature: (note: Note, max_chars: int=6000, overlap_chars: int=300) -> List[Section]

modules.summary: Frontmatter parsing and normalization helpers.
Note identity resolution: frontmatter `id` (ULID) with a path-hash fallback.

functions.name: resolve_note_id
functions.doc: Frontmatter `id` (any non-empty scalar, stripped) if present, else hash_string(relative_path).

modules.summary: Load Markdown notes from the vault into ``Note`` objects.

functions.name: ignore_tags
functions.doc: Tags that mean "never index this note". Configurable: `vault.ignore_tags`.

functions.name: skip_dirs
functions.doc: Vault-relative directory names that are never indexed.

functions.name: has_ignore_frontmatter_tag
functions.doc: True when a frontmatter tag is `ignore`/`secret` (with or without `#`).

functions.name: is_skipped_path
functions.doc: True when any parent directory is skipped or hidden.

vault_spider.index.role: Builds and maintains the ChromaDB collection (one document entry + N section entries per note, distinguished by the `granularity` metadata field) with failure-safe incremental sync; plus a read-only reader for the Streamlit UI.

modules.summary: Read-only Chroma access for Vault Spider.
Build and maintain a Chroma collection for Markdown notes.

classes.name: IndexStore
classes.doc: Maintain the persistent vector store and in-memory BM25 indexes.

vault_spider.retrieval.role: Hybrid search: BM25 + embeddings fused by pure scoring functions, optional rerank (thorough mode), an on-disk query-embedding cache, and assembly of the retrieval output contract (evidence/citations).

modules.summary: Assemble the retrieval output contract (the citation/evidence JSON).

functions.name: build_retrieval_output
functions.signature: (query: str, mode: str, granularity: str, rows: List[Dict[str, object]]) -> Dict[str, object]

modules.summary: Score-fusion functions for hybrid retrieval (pure, no ranker state).

functions.name: reciprocal_rank_fusion

functions.name: zscore_sigmoid_fusion
functions.signature: (semantic_scores: pd.Series, keyword_scores: pd.Series, allowed_ids: Optional[Collection[str]]=None, temperature: float=1.0, weight: float=0.5, eps: float=1e-08) -> pd.DataFrame

modules.summary: Best-effort on-disk cache for query embeddings.

vault_spider.synthesis.role: Turns a retrieval output contract into an LLM-synthesized, cited answer; abstains when the notes lack the answer.

vault_spider.enrich.role: App-agnostic enrichment planner: retrieves a note's neighborhood and proposes title/frontmatter/links/placement as JSON. Never mutates files or the index; plans are applied via the mutation commands.

modules.summary: App-agnostic enrichment planner.

functions.name: gather_neighbors

functions.name: postprocess
functions.signature: (parsed: Optional[Dict[str, Any]], inp: EnrichInput, neighbors: List[Dict[str, Any]]) -> Dict[str, Any]

vault_spider.obsidian.role: The write path: contract-enforcing note mutations executed through the official Obsidian CLI (app must be running, macOS only), plus the macOS vault registry bridge. Mutation code never writes vault files directly.

modules.summary: Safe Obsidian vault mutations, executed through the running Obsidian app.
Invocation layer for the official Obsidian CLI (the mutation backend).

functions.signature: (path: str, expected_content: str, content: str) -> None
functions.doc: Atomically refuse a write unless Obsidian still has the expected note text.

vault_spider.evaluation.role: Golden-dataset benchmark: strict label validation against the corpus, then scored retrieval (nDCG@k, evidence recall, complete@k, MRR) and optional LLM-judged synthesis runs. Always run against a dedicated --chroma-path.

modules.summary: Golden-dataset evaluation: label validation and scored benchmark runs.
Load and validate a golden evaluation dataset (manifest, queries, corpus labels).

classes.name: DatasetError
classes.doc: The dataset cannot be loaded at all (missing or unparseable files).

classes.name: DatasetNotFoundError
classes.doc: The --dataset path (or its dataset.yaml) does not exist.

functions.name: load_dataset
functions.doc: Parse the manifest and query file strictly; semantic checks live in validate().

functions.name: corpus_labels
functions.doc: Corpus notes exactly as sync sees them, keyed by vault-relative path.

functions.name: validate
functions.doc: Semantic validation: labels resolve, contracts hold, counts match.

modules.summary: Execute a validated golden dataset against the index and score the results.

files.summary: Shared pytest fixtures: a network-free FakeProvider and a tiny vault.
Tests for tools/backfill.py.
Tests for vault_spider.corpus.chunker.split_sections.
Tests for the vault-spider CLI, driven in-process with a fake provider.
Tests for vault_spider.compounding.distill (synthesize --save).
Tests for vault_spider.enrich.planner.
Tests for the eval commands: dataset validation, scoring, and the run loop.
Tests for vault_spider.retrieval.evidence and the mixed-mode per-note cap.
Tests for vault_spider.corpus.frontmatter (parsing + normalization).
Golden-value tests for vault_spider.retrieval.fusion.
Tests for vault_spider.corpus.identity.
Tests for vault_spider.compounding.lint.
Tests for vault_spider.corpus.loader (Note loading, date resolution, doc text).
Network-free tests for the MCP adapter and generated tool contracts.
Tests for the note-mutation commands (no Obsidian; the backend is faked).
Tests for vault_spider.llm.openrouter request/response hardening (no network).
Tests for the launchd maintenance runner without a real vault or provider.
Tests for the provenance field: loading, filtering, synthesis framing,
Tests for Obsidian registry parsing and read-path root fallback.
Tests for vault_spider.settings (the optional config.yaml).
Tests for the launchd plist renderer; no real service is loaded.
Tests for scripts/setup_obsidian.py (no network and no real Obsidian).
Tests for vault_spider.index.store.IndexStore.sync against a real Chroma db.
Tests for vault_spider.synthesis.answer.
Tests for pure helpers in vault_spider.utils.
Smoke tests for the stable vault-spider wrapper entrypoint.

Agent skill for operating the vault (plus references/ for capture, commands, eval/server).
